title: 1.3: Includes a runtime log message showing the webserver reached a ready state, such as server ready logs or workers spawned messages.
1.4: Includes a runtime log message showing the background worker reached a ready state, such as a qcluster log reporting ready for work.
1.5: Includes a runtime log message showing the document consumer entered an idle watching state, such as directory monitoring confirmation or file watching ready logs.
1.6: Mentions a runtime health check confirming Redis availability such as PING response or connection confirmation.
1.7: Mentions the execution of at least one scheduled background task during idle time, such as a scheduler log showing task creation.
1.8: States that the system remained idle during ongoing background activity, such as periodic scheduler logs appearing during periods of zero document ingestion events.
1.9: Includes a runtime log sequence showing reconnection to the message broker, such as PONG response or task enqueue confirmation logs.
1.10: Includes a runtime log message indicating workers returned to a ready state after restart, such as scheduler logs reporting workers ready for work
1.11: States the frequency or interval of periodic background tasks, such as "mail check every 10 minutes" or "hourly classifier training".
2.1: Claims static file reading suffices such as 'qcluster schedules are defined in settings.py'.
2.2: Shows Redis command syntax such as SET "django_q:key" "value" or BLPOP "queue" "1".
